# `energy/state_per_capita_availability_kwh`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: State-wise per-capita availability of power  
**One-line**: Annual per-capita electricity availability (kWh / person / year), by state and Union Territory, fiscal year.  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_per_capita_availability_kwh.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_per_capita_availability_kwh.json)

## Definition

Annual per-capita electricity availability (kWh / person / year), by state and Union Territory, fiscal year. Computed by CEA as state energy availability (MU) ÷ state mid-year population. The most citizen-relevant single number for 'how much power do people in this state actually get to use' — captures both supply expansion and transmission losses. The all-India figure roughly tripled from ~600 kWh in FY05 to ~1300 kWh in FY24, but the inter-state spread is wide (Goa / Punjab / Gujarat above 2,000; Bihar / Manipur / Nagaland still below 400).

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2004-04..2024-04 (21 periods)
- **Spatial**: India (states + UTs); 35 entities (35 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 722

## Methodology vintage

RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian States 2024-25 edition; originating data Central Electricity Authority, Ministry of Power.

## Notes

Source: RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian States 2024-25 edition, Table 138. Originating data: Central Electricity Authority, Ministry of Power. Per-capita is on geographical-area population (resident), not consumption-weighted — large industrial-export states (e.g. Chhattisgarh, Sikkim) post inflated numbers because the power they generate is consumed elsewhere.
